jokes.text: Yeah, I discovered a new back muscle to work out. Ladies, you'll thank me come tank top season.
jokes.setup: Asked about his break activities
jokes.punchline: Troy's oblivious confidence about his physique impressing women
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy - earnest but misguided confidence. Well-structured setup/payoff with good character voice.

jokes.text: I checked all of Pierce's wardrobes for portals to magical worlds. All I found was something called Emmanuelle in Space on laserdisc... I couldn't watch it, but it sounded sexy.
jokes.setup: Abed's matter-of-fact delivery about searching for Narnia-style portals
jokes.punchline: Finding Pierce's porn collection instead, delivered with characteristic Abed deadpan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - pop culture reference mixed with innocent delivery about finding porn. The 'laserdisc' detail adds extra character specificity.

jokes.text: Laserdisc.
jokes.setup: Abed mentions finding Emmanuelle in Space on laserdisc
jokes.punchline: Pierce's proud/defensive single-word response about his outdated technology
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word that captures Pierce's defensiveness about being outdated. Classic reaction beat.

jokes.text: Somebody's finding river fingers with a cute boy.
jokes.setup: Annie describes volunteer work in overly sweet voice
jokes.punchline: Shirley's knowing observation that combines the gruesome detail with romance
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition of romantic and macabre. Shirley's maternal intuition delivered with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Is it the guy that looks like Anderson Cooper but with a soul patch and the ponytail? / No. / Is it black Michael Chiklis? / No. / The white George Foreman? / You guys are talking about the same person.
jokes.setup: Group guessing who Annie's crush is with increasingly specific descriptions
jokes.punchline: Pierce's realization they're all describing one biracial person badly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdity that builds to Pierce accidentally being the voice of reason. Perfect Community-style racial humor that's clever, not cheap.

jokes.text: Chang the subject.
jokes.setup: Annie says 'Change the subject'
jokes.punchline: Immediately corrects herself to make a Chang pun
jokes.explanation: Predictable pun but Annie's self-correction shows the infectious nature of Chang's bad wordplay.

jokes.text: That was before he started using his name as a pun. It makes me so changry.
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining why they won't let Chang in
jokes.punchline: Jeff accidentally does a Chang pun while complaining about Chang puns
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Jeff falls victim to what he's criticizing. Shows how Chang's influence is spreading.

jokes.text: Oh, God, it's happening to me.
jokes.setup: Jeff realizes he just made a Chang pun
jokes.punchline: His horror at his own linguistic infection
jokes.explanation: Jeff's self-awareness and horror is perfectly in character. Good follow-up to the pun joke.

jokes.text: Is it Fat Neil? / Blue Streak? / Mud Flaps? / Okay, even I know some of these are Transformers.
jokes.setup: Group continues guessing Annie's crush with increasingly absurd names
jokes.punchline: Abed's meta-observation that they've moved from people to Transformers
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed meta-commentary. The escalation from real people to fictional robots is absurd and perfectly observed.

jokes.text: I agree with brown Jamie Lee Curtis.
jokes.setup: Shirley makes a reasonable point about learning names
jokes.punchline: Pierce agrees while demonstrating exactly the opposite behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - trying to be agreeable while being completely inappropriate. The irony is sharp.

jokes.text: I hope he didn't drive his car into another marathon.
jokes.setup: Duncan being late to class
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Duncan's implied history of drunk driving disasters
jokes.explanation: Dark callback that treats serious drunk driving as casual character trait. Very Community tone.

jokes.text: Well, forgive me for being forgiving. Oh, that's right. You don't know how.
jokes.setup: Britta criticizes Shirley for taking back her cheating ex
jokes.punchline: Shirley's passive-aggressive comeback about Britta's inability to forgive
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Shirley's Christian passive-aggression delivered with perfect bite.

jokes.text: I wish my mouth was further away from my brain. I wish my brain had its own brain.
jokes.setup: Troy regretting speaking without thinking
jokes.punchline: Escalating absurd solutions that make no logical sense
jokes.explanation: Pure Troy logic - sweet, stupid, and surprisingly philosophical. The escalation from physical to metaphysical is perfect.

jokes.text: You have my word as your roommate, and that is a bond I will never violate.
jokes.setup: Troy asking Abed to keep a secret
jokes.punchline: Abed's overly formal, contract-like response to a simple request
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed - turning casual friendship into formal agreement. The delivery is perfectly deadpan.

jokes.text: You can't tweet it either. / We never discussed new media.
jokes.setup: Troy adding social media restriction to secret-keeping
jokes.punchline: Abed's literal interpretation of their agreement's scope
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy/Abed dynamic - Troy's modern concern met with Abed's legalistic precision. Great character interplay.

jokes.text: I have stopped drinking. Mainly due to the fact that I could no longer get an erection.
jokes.setup: Duncan announcing positive life change of sobriety
jokes.punchline: Immediately oversharing deeply personal medical information
jokes.explanation: Classic Duncan overshare. The whiplash from inspirational to TMI is perfectly executed cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Now that I'm on the wagon, you can expect both this class and my penis to be more focused and rewarding.
jokes.setup: Duncan continuing his oversharing about sobriety benefits
jokes.punchline: Comparing academic improvement to sexual function improvement
jokes.explanation: Duncan doubles down on the inappropriate comparison. The parallel structure makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I am so sorry. I train seeing-eye dogs, and today was graduation. I couldn't leave without shaking all their little paws.
jokes.setup: Rich apologizing for being late
jokes.punchline: Impossibly wholesome reason involving dog graduation ceremony
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rich introduction - so absurdly wholesome it's suspicious. The detail about shaking paws is chef's kiss perfect.

jokes.text: He has a landline and uses the word 'album.'
jokes.setup: Discussing Rich's age
jokes.punchline: Annie's specific evidence of Rich being old-fashioned
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about generational markers. Economical and specific details.

jokes.text: By that logic, I wouldn't have a shot with anybody at this table.
jokes.setup: Group discussing age gaps in dating
jokes.punchline: Pierce's inadvertent admission that he's too old for everyone
jokes.explanation: Pierce accidentally revealing his romantic delusions. Good character moment but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Right? Britta, you're always talking about how it's weird we have no one of Latin descent in our group. / Si.
jokes.setup: Annie manipulating the group with fake diversity concerns
jokes.punchline: Britta's stereotypical 'Si' response proving Annie's point wrong
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Annie's manipulation exposed by Britta's performative wokeness. The 'Si' is perfect.

jokes.text: And, Pierce, you're always looking for someone old enough to find your racism subversive.
jokes.setup: Annie continuing her fake reasoning for adding Rich
jokes.punchline: Brutally accurate assessment of Pierce's nostalgic racism
jokes.explanation: Devastating character insight delivered casually. Annie's manipulation is surgical and the Pierce read is perfect.

jokes.text: No, I'd say you got about two more good years.
jokes.setup: Andre saying he's finished (meaning done talking)
jokes.punchline: Britta misinterpreting as him aging out of attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and brutal honesty from Britta. The misunderstanding is organic and the burn is severe.

jokes.text: Britta, I'm a grown-ass woman, and I made my decision.
jokes.setup: Britta questioning Shirley's relationship choices
jokes.punchline: Shirley using profanity to assert her maturity
jokes.explanation: Shirley's rare profanity to assert independence is perfectly in character and surprising.

jokes.text: Because I'm pregnant, okay?
jokes.setup: Britta pushing Shirley about her relationship decision
jokes.punchline: Shirley dropping a bombshell to end the argument
jokes.explanation: Major plot reveal that silences opposition. Not funny per se, but creates comedy through awkward silence.

jokes.text: Halloween. / What? / Both: Nothing.
jokes.setup: Shirley announces 8-week pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Troy blurts out the conception timeline, then everyone covers
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and awkward cover-up. The synchronized 'Nothing' is comedy gold.

jokes.text: So help yourselves to tropical skittles, cigarettes, or mouthwash.
jokes.setup: Jeff apologizing for poor mixer refreshments
jokes.punchline: List of inappropriate vending machine options as party food
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from weird to inappropriate party snacks. Captures Greendale's low-rent nature.

jokes.text: Nature's Viagra. Right?
jokes.setup: Troy trying to subtly investigate the timing of Shirley's conception
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to discuss seasonal horniness
jokes.explanation: Troy's innocent attempt at being subtle about sex creates maximum awkwardness - perfect character moment

jokes.text: His mind is like a fortress.
jokes.setup: Jeff asking about Troy's interrogation of Andre
jokes.punchline: Troy's dramatic assessment of his complete failure
jokes.explanation: Troy's dramatic language for a simple concept is perfectly in character. Great economy.

jokes.text: Pierce, if someone had sex with Chang and they get to not remember that happened, that's a gift from God.
jokes.setup: Pierce suggesting they tell Shirley about Chang
jokes.punchline: Troy's earnest theological interpretation of memory loss
jokes.explanation: Troy's innocent wisdom about sexual trauma recovery is both funny and surprisingly insightful.

jokes.text: Now imagine your teeth are a uterus and that parsley is a half-Chinese baby.
jokes.setup: Pierce making an analogy about telling people about embarrassing things
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely inappropriate metaphor mixing food and pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Peak Pierce absurdity. Completely inappropriate but delivered with such confidence it's horrifyingly funny.

jokes.text: I love Star Wars. That's Troy. I love footballs.
jokes.setup: Quendra mistakes Abed for Troy
jokes.punchline: Troy's correction that reduces his entire identity to loving footballs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment - his self-identification is comically reductive and innocent, parallel to Abed's Star Wars obsession

jokes.text: My name is Quendra, and I spell it with a Q-U. / I love Star Wars. / That's Troy. / I love footballs.
jokes.setup: Quendra introducing herself to Abed
jokes.punchline: Troy's overly simple, plural response to match her energy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment - trying to match someone's energy with hilarious oversimplification.

jokes.text: I spell kettle corn with a Q-U. / Well, don't.
jokes.setup: Quendra applying her quirky spelling to everything
jokes.punchline: Jeff's blunt shutdown of her affectation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff deadpan response to annoying quirk. Economy is flawless.

jokes.text: We didn't know how to reach you. / That's not a true. That's a lie. / It's a mixer. We didn't know how to reach you. / It's a mixer. It's a mixer. It's a mixer.
jokes.setup: Chang confronting them about not being invited
jokes.punchline: Chang's breakdown into repetitive mantra
jokes.explanation: Chang's mental breakdown is perfectly in character. The repetition builds to genuine uncomfortable comedy.

jokes.text: Works every time.
jokes.setup: Chang having an apparent breakdown
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was a calculated manipulation tactic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang moment - revealing his apparent insanity is actually strategic. Great misdirection.

jokes.text: You can't talk and then do a slow clap. / You don't know that.
jokes.setup: Chang doing dramatic gesture while speaking
jokes.punchline: Chang defending his right to break dramatic conventions
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on dramatic conventions. Chang's defiance of storytelling rules is perfect.

jokes.text: Unless you want a front row seat to Annie loves Richie, it's time to Chang your point of view.
jokes.setup: Chang making his case to Jeff
jokes.punchline: Another Chang pun, this time more elaborate
jokes.explanation: Standard Chang pun but in service of plot advancement. The rhyming makes it slightly more elaborate.

jokes.text: It's not even clever. You keep using it as the word change.
jokes.setup: Jeff hearing another Chang pun
jokes.punchline: Jeff pointing out the technical failure of the wordplay
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about bad puns. Jeff's criticism is technically accurate and funny.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm serious, baby. I am yahoo serious. I'm serious F.M. Welcome to the world serious of seriousness, sponsored by honey nut serios.
jokes.setup: Chang questioning if Jeff is serious
jokes.punchline: Jeff's elaborate escalating serious/cereal puns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating wordplay that builds momentum. Yahoo Serious reference is perfectly obscure for Jeff.

jokes.text: No, Annie, in fact, you're gonna wish I was stalling... Oh, I just got it. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Annie asks if Jeff is stalling
jokes.punchline: Jeff's delayed recognition that he actually was stalling
jokes.explanation: Jeff's self-aware moment of realizing his own behavior - good character insight delivered with comedy timing

jokes.text: I want to say some names to you. Jeffrey Dahmer. Ted Bundy. Rich.
jokes.setup: Jeff building dramatic tension with serial killer names
jokes.punchline: Including innocent Rich in the list
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and escalation. The inclusion of Rich with serial killers is absurd and brilliant.

jokes.text: When he eats, he holds his fork like a murderer's knife, gnawing at its skewered payload like a deranged woodland rodent.
jokes.setup: Jeff listing Chang's disturbing qualities
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description of Chang's eating habits
jokes.explanation: Peak Jeff rhetoric. The imagery is vivid and absurd, perfectly capturing Chang's unsettling nature.

jokes.text: When he eats, he holds his fork like a murderer's knife, gnawing at its skewered payload like a deranged woodland rodent.
jokes.setup: Jeff cataloging Chang's disturbing behaviors
jokes.punchline: Elaborate description of Chang's eating habits with vivid imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and vivid description that makes normal eating habits sound genuinely disturbing - great imagery

jokes.text: This number eight scoop of vanilla tapioca with a Ph.D in being swell and a master's in 'everybody loves me'?
jokes.setup: Jeff describing Rich's suspicious perfection
jokes.punchline: Academic degrees in being likeable, mixed with bland dessert metaphor
jokes.explanation: Jeff's rhetoric reaches peak absurdity. The vanilla tapioca metaphor is inspired and specific.

jokes.text: Nobody is this good a person. And nobody can get any worse than this.
jokes.setup: Jeff's entire speech comparing Rich and Chang
jokes.punchline: Final argument that known evil is better than suspicious good
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of Jeff's argument. The logic is twisted but compelling, pure Jeff Winger.

jokes.text: If you don't vote for Rich, I'll shatter your world. / You're gonna what? / I'll shatter your world. / I heard you. I vote for Chang.
jokes.setup: Pierce threatening Shirley with mysterious consequence
jokes.punchline: Shirley's defiant response despite not understanding the threat
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Shirley's defiance in the face of unclear threat is very her.

jokes.text: Chang and Shirley had sex on Halloween. You may be pregnant with his baby.
jokes.setup: Pierce's threat to shatter Shirley's world
jokes.punchline: The devastating revelation delivered with brutal directness
jokes.explanation: Massive plot revelation delivered with perfect economy and timing - completely changes the episode's stakes

jokes.text: She crossed me.
jokes.setup: Pierce drops the Chang-Shirley bombshell
jokes.punchline: His simple, mob-like justification for the betrayal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce - treats voting against him like a personal betrayal worthy of nuclear retaliation, delivered with gangster logic

jokes.text: Oh, now I understand these photos. Do you mind if I forward these to my brother?
jokes.setup: Rich learning about Chang and Shirley's Halloween encounter
jokes.punchline: Rich's inappropriate response to compromising photos
jokes.explanation: Reveals Rich might not be as perfect as he seems. Cringe moment that serves character development.

jokes.text: Kettle corn!
jokes.setup: Tense dramatic moment with group in crisis
jokes.punchline: Rich's oblivious cheerful snack announcement
jokes.explanation: Perfect tonal whiplash. Rich's oblivious cheerfulness amid drama is absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: Mezzanine?
jokes.setup: Jeff looking for Andre in an unusual location
jokes.punchline: Single word that suggests he's searching architectural features
jokes.explanation: Absurd single-word joke. Perfect economy but limited impact.

jokes.text: You ever have something that you didn't truly appreciate until you didn't have it anymore? / Oh, yeah. Keristina. / Old girlfriend? / Almond facial scrub. They only make it in Finland.
jokes.setup: Andre's heartfelt question about lost love
jokes.punchline: Jeff's emotional attachment to discontinued skincare product
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff moment. The misdirection from girlfriend to cosmetics is brilliant character work.

jokes.text: And if it's Chang's, we'll go for golf.
jokes.setup: Andre saying he'll love the baby regardless of paternity
jokes.punchline: His casual acceptance with sport-based racial stereotyping
jokes.explanation: Andre's casual racism delivered with genuine love - shows he's trying but still has problematic assumptions

jokes.text: And if it's Chang's, we'll go for golf.
jokes.setup: Andre discussing potential parentage of baby
jokes.punchline: Casual racist assumption about Asian athletic ability
jokes.explanation: Casual racism that reveals character but relies on stereotype. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You are the strangest, coolest, most genuine person I've ever met, and the thing that scares me about you is how good you make me wish I was.
jokes.setup: Jeff appearing to have genuine emotional breakthrough with Rich
jokes.punchline: The sincerity that becomes manipulative by being too perfect
jokes.explanation: Jeff's manipulation through apparent vulnerability - sounds genuine but is calculated to achieve his goal

jokes.text: Help me, Rich. Help me become like you. I mean, I am so amazing. But I'm not perfect. You are. Give me that power. So I can abuse it.
jokes.setup: Jeff's seemingly sincere request for self-improvement
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing his intention to abuse any goodness he gains
jokes.explanation: Peak Jeff Winger - brutally honest about his own moral corruption. The escalation from humble to narcissistic to evil is perfect.

jokes.text: I mean, I am so amazing. But I'm not perfect. You are. Give me that power. So I can abuse it.
jokes.setup: Jeff's seemingly sincere plea to Rich
jokes.punchline: His complete honesty about wanting to abuse Rich's goodness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jeff moment - he's so manipulative he's honest about being manipulative. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: You can't just fake being good in order to get away with doing bad things.
jokes.setup: Rich responding to Jeff's manipulation attempt
jokes.punchline: Rich perfectly diagnosing Jeff's entire life philosophy
jokes.explanation: Rich cuts through Jeff's manipulation with perfect clarity - shows why genuinely good people are Jeff's kryptonite

jokes.text: You can't just fake being good in order to get away with doing bad things. / Oh, I completely understand. And do you understand that I still have to try?
jokes.setup: Rich explaining moral philosophy to Jeff
jokes.punchline: Jeff acknowledging the lesson while completely ignoring it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff logic - understanding moral principles while being completely amoral. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: And if you don't help me, you're a bad person.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to manipulate Rich into helping him
jokes.punchline: Using moral blackmail to achieve immoral goals
jokes.explanation: Jeff's twisted logic taken to its extreme. Perfect manipulation technique that's both effective and morally bankrupt.

jokes.text: Well, it's a secret ingredient...a couple extra pinches of love.
jokes.setup: Rich explaining what makes his kettle corn special
jokes.punchline: Impossibly wholesome answer that seems almost suspicious
jokes.explanation: Rich's perfect wholesomeness is almost unsettling. The sincerity makes it funny through contrast.

jokes.text: Rich, you know they're doing a fake morning show? There are no cameras. / I know. It's just a fun way to start the day.
jokes.setup: Jeff pointing out the absurdity of the fake TV show
jokes.punchline: Rich's acknowledgment that he knows it's fake but enjoys it anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rich moment - so genuinely positive he participates in fake joy. Makes his goodness seem almost alien.
